Gwynn Review - Empires & Puzzles Construct Hero Strategy Guide

Is this little robot a bee or a drill? Doesn't matter—Gwynn stings. We review the Construct family's DoT specialist who bypasses minions and turns up the heat on burns and poisons. Find out if his stats still hold up in 2026.
Gwynn: The Construct Stinger Who Melts Minions & Boosts Burns
Gwynn, The High-Voltage Drill Bit: Minion Bypass & DoT Amplification

Meet Gwynn, the little Construct that could. Released back in mid-2024, this Holy (Yellow) 5-star hero from the Covenant Summon brings a unique mix of direct damage and lingering pain. Visually resembling a cross between a bumblebee and a high-powered mining drill, Gwynn's main gimmick is punishing enemies with burns while simultaneously making
burns and poisons hurt significantly more. While he might look small, his "High Energy Field" packs a wallop, especially against minion-heavy teams.

Gwynn is... adorable? Terrifying? It's hard to say. He looks like a bronze-plated insect knight wielding a lance that’s definitely compensating for something. The "drill-bit helmet" design fits the Construct theme perfectly—industrial, magical, and ready to pierce through defenses. The warm amber and gold palette signals his Holy alignment, but those glowing eyes suggest there's a burning furnace inside. He’s definitely the "scrappy little fighter" of the Construct family compared to the more imposing figures like Nautica.

If you pulled Gwynn, rejoice! He is an excellent carry hero for map stages (hitting all enemies is great for farming) and will serve you well in Raids up to Diamond. Prioritize leveling his attack.

Gwynn is a niche tool for your War chest. Don't use your Alpha Aethers on him unless you are specifically building a DoT tournament team or lack a modern yellow hit-all. He is a solid "Second War Team" hero, but not a primary defender anymore.

Gwynn is like a classic sports car in a world of futuristic hover-tanks. He might not have the raw horsepower (stats) of the 2026 models, but his handling (mechanics) is still superb. The ability to bypass minions and amplify DoT damage gives him a unique role that doesn't go obsolete as quickly as pure damage dealers. If you like watching enemy health bars turn red, purple, and melt away, Gwynn is your guy.
: Gwynn currently has no costume, but he is a prime candidate for one in the future to fix those aging stats. Fingers crossed!
: Solid Utility Attacker. Keep him polished and ready for Minion Wars.
